close_reason: Fixed put() docstring to describe atomic rename pattern
description: lib.rs:104 says 'the file is created or truncated with mode 0600'. The actual mechanism is: a new file with mode 0600 is created (create_new), then renamed over the destination, replacing the inode. There is no 'truncation' of the existing file — the existing inode is unlinked when its last reference (the directory entry) is replaced by rename. This wording matters for two reasons: (a) hard-linked secret files lose the link on put (existing link still points to old inode); (b) opens-for-read concurrent with put see a consistent old-or-new file because the inode is replaced atomically, NOT because of truncation. Reword to: 'the file is replaced atomically by a new inode with mode 0600'.
title: [chore] put() docstring 'created or truncated with mode 0600' is technically inaccurate — file is replaced via rename
